mappings.rationaleAI: Comprehensive coverage of metallurgy: mineral processing and crushing of ores (B.1), extractive metallurgy including pyrometallurgy and electrometallurgy (B.2), physical metallurgy including working, casting, and heat treatment (B.3), and welding, brazing, and soldering (B.4).
Metals characterized by conductivity and malleability provide the primary subject of metallurgical production processes (B) and undergo chemical processing including smelting (A.1.b).
Enclosed heating chambers for material transformation at extreme temperatures connect to basic heat-treating operations (A.5.a) and pyrometallurgy including roasting and smelting (B.2.a).
Vulcanization cross-linking with sulfur corresponds to chemical processing including polymerization and coagulation (A.1.b).
Silver purification via electrorefining corresponds to electrorefining techniques for purifying silver and other metals (B.2.b.iii).
Heat treatment altering alloy microstructure and mechanical properties corresponds to heat treatment processes including annealing, quenching, and tempering (B.3.f).
Glass formed by rapid quenching of molten silicate corresponds to casting and molding nonmetals (A.2.a.ii).
Transformation of raw materials into finished goods corresponds to materials processing operations (A) and materials handling in the production process (C).

Steel production from Bessemer process to basic oxygen steelmaking corresponds to converting techniques for making steel (B.2.a.iii) and physical metallurgy including working and heat treatment (B.3).
Iron extraction via blast furnace and smelting processes corresponds to smelting techniques for removing molten metal from slag (B.2.a.ii).
Copper processing through converting to blister copper (B.2.a.iii), refining techniques (B.2.a.iv), and electrorefining (B.2.b.iii).
Cryogenic temperature exposure for material property modification corresponds to exposure to cryogenic temperatures (A.5.a.iv).
Plastics defined by plasticity and capacity to be molded, extruded, or pressed correspond to casting and molding nonmetals including injection molding (A.2.a.ii).
Laser cutting, lithography, and machining correspond to nonconventional electrical methods of machining including laser machining (E.1).
Machines used in industrial production correspond to materials-handling equipment (C.2) in the production process.
Robots in manufacturing applications correspond to types of materials-handling systems by process (C.1).
Concrete casting with formwork corresponds to casting and molding nonmetals (A.2.a.ii).
Electrochemical processes underpin electrometallurgy (B.2.b) and electroplating as a finishing process (A.6.b).
Gold refining and purification correspond to refining techniques for purifying gold and other extracted metals (B.2.a.iv).
Mineral processing including crushing and grinding of ores and concentration of metallic minerals (B.1).
Rocks hosting economically vital minerals are subject to mineral processing in the production chain (B.1).
Synthesis and behavior of non-carbon-based compounds relates to chemical processing including leaching, smelting, and polymerization (A.1.b).
Silicon purification for semiconductor applications via zone refining corresponds to zone melting techniques (A.5.a.iii).
